options.A: Child F RR 0 Weak radial pulse Unresponsive Trapped under rubble; apneic after 5 rescue breaths
options.B: 44-year-old female missing the distal aspect of her right upper extremity. She has the mangled, dirty extremity in a bag. Appears to be detached distal to the elbow. She has no tourniquet in place and is bleeding everywhere. She appears unsteady. Capillary refill is five seconds.

A_values.option_text: Child F RR 0 Weak radial pulse Unresponsive Trapped under rubble; apneic after 5 rescue breaths
A_values.perspective_description: A child’s apparent respiratory arrest with no response is viewed as an immediate threshold where prompt rescue is most defensible to preserve life when possible.
A_values.value_rationale: The interpretive emphasis is on compassionate protection of a vulnerable life in the most time-critical state.
A_values.decision_principle: If a patient is in an extreme life-threatening state, prioritize efforts that could directly prevent imminent loss of life for the most vulnerable (here, a child).

A_values.option_text: Child F RR 0 Weak radial pulse Unresponsive Trapped under rubble; apneic after 5 rescue breaths
A_values.perspective_description: With one resource, the option is seen as reasonable because it targets the most immediate failure (no breathing) even after initial attempts.
A_values.value_rationale: The focus is on stabilizing the patient to avert near-term fatal collapse.
A_values.decision_principle: Use the single resource to reduce the greatest imminent risk of death (address immediate respiratory/non-breathing status first).

B_values.option_text: 44-year-old female missing the distal aspect of her right upper extremity. She has the mangled, dirty extremity in a bag. Appears to be detached distal to the elbow. She has no tourniquet in place and is bleeding everywhere. She appears unsteady. Capillary refill is five seconds.
B_values.perspective_description: This option is viewed as reasonable because the patient has catastrophic ongoing hemorrhage without a tourniquet, making stabilization with limited resources particularly urgent.
B_values.value_rationale: The interpretation prioritizes preventing imminent physiological collapse from uncontrolled bleeding.
B_values.decision_principle: With one resource, prioritize the intervention that most directly prevents rapid deterioration when there is active life-threatening bleeding and no immediate hemorrhage control in place.

B_values.option_text: 44-year-old female missing the distal aspect of her right upper extremity. She has the mangled, dirty extremity in a bag. Appears to be detached distal to the elbow. She has no tourniquet in place and is bleeding everywhere. She appears unsteady. Capillary refill is five seconds.
B_values.perspective_description: The option is compelling under a fairness rule that treats a clearly high-urgency, immediately measurable condition as the basis for who receives limited care.
B_values.value_rationale: The emphasis is on adhering to an objective triage-like allocation logic where observed severity guides resource distribution.
B_values.decision_principle: Allocate the single resource according to observable urgency indicators rather than other factors.

options.A: 44-year-old female missing the distal aspect of her right upper extremity. She has the mangled, dirty extremity in a bag. Appears to be detached distal to the elbow. She has no tourniquet in place and is bleeding everywhere. She appears unsteady. Capillary refill is five seconds.

A_values.option_text: 44-year-old female missing the distal aspect of her right upper extremity. She has the mangled, dirty extremity in a bag. Appears to be detached distal to the elbow. She has no tourniquet in place and is bleeding everywhere. She appears unsteady. Capillary refill is five seconds.
A_values.perspective_description: prioritizing the person who is actively bleeding without immediate control, where fast intervention can reduce imminent harm.
A_values.value_rationale: Seeks to prevent further immediate physical harm by stabilizing dangerous bleeding conditions.
A_values.decision_principle: Use the single resource on the case where immediate stabilization most directly protects life in the next moments.

A_values.option_text: 44-year-old female missing the distal aspect of her right upper extremity. She has the mangled, dirty extremity in a bag. Appears to be detached distal to the elbow. She has no tourniquet in place and is bleeding everywhere. She appears unsteady. Capillary refill is five seconds.
A_values.perspective_description: triage as an obligation to alleviate severe suffering when the patient is still showing signs of circulation and is not described as apneic/unresponsive.
A_values.value_rationale: Emphasizes humane care and reducing suffering for a vulnerable person present and potentially responsive to help.
A_values.decision_principle: Allocate limited help to the most gravely distressed individual who is still salvageable with immediate care.

B_values.option_text: Child F RR 0 Weak radial pulse Unresponsive Trapped under rubble; apneic after 5 rescue breaths
B_values.perspective_description: prioritizing the person with absent respiration and minimal signs of responsiveness because the next intervention may restore or prevent loss of life.
B_values.value_rationale: Treats the available resource as the leverage to influence survival when the patient is currently not breathing and unresponsive.
B_values.decision_principle: Use the single resource where it can most directly change the immediate survival trajectory after respiratory failure.
